role_title: Manager, Executive Social Strategy & Operations Lead

level_signals: 6+ years of experience building and scaling social-first narratives
Comp range $136K–$200K base consistent with senior IC or manager level at DoorDash
Advises and pushes back on senior leaders; cross-functional orchestration role

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6+ years of experience building and scaling social-first narratives for a high-visibility company, product, or principal, and owning strategy, voice, drafting, and distribution (especially on X).
You have a proven ability to grow and sustain a credible presence on X (brand or principal) with measurable distribution outcomes.
You have exceptional writing and editing (you can write like a human, in an executive voice, at speed).
You have strong judgment coupled with a high risk tolerance (you understand second-order effects, escalation, and how to operate under public-company constraints).
You have deep curiosity about technology and the economy (you are comfortable with finance and policy audiences and technically fluent conversations).
You have an operator mindset (you don't just post, you build a system--formats, pipeline, measurement, and iteration).
You're comfortable pushing back on senior leaders with clarity and good instincts.
X is the primary platform. YouTube, Substack, Threads, and LinkedIn are secondary channels.

explicitly_excluded: Traditional comms or PR experience is not required.

scope.surface: Executive and corporate social media presence, primarily on X; emerging initiatives including agentic commerce, autonomous robotics, and in-store software
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B marketplace/logistics platform (local commerce)
primary_metrics_inferred: Audience growth for founder, executive, and corporate accounts
Engagement quality beyond likes (learning loop outcomes)
Credibility and influence with technical talent, policymakers, market observers, and prospective partners
core_responsibilities: Provide strategic and tactical counsel to executives on their social media presence, specifically on X
Support the social media presence for several founder, executive, and corporate accounts: build a repeatable content pipeline, draft posts/threads, and advise when to engage (and when not to)
Assess existing and new accounts: network mapping, engagement strategy, amplification plans, and experiments to help the right stories travel to the right people
Launch new accounts and build their audiences as needed, defining the voice, content franchises, guardrails, and distribution operating system
Translate complex topics into platform-native thinking (earnings context, AI strategy, autonomy, regulation, marketplaces, our technology roadmap)
'Build in public' for emerging initiatives: share progress without over-sharing (partner with Product/Engineering to package demos, milestones, and learnings)
Work cross-functionally with Brand Social, Policy, Legal, IR, and Employer Brand to move quickly while staying accurate
Build a learning loop: define what 'good' looks like (beyond likes), track outcomes, run post-mortems, and refine strategy

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($136K–$200K base) with equity upside
Role is clearly differentiated from traditional PR/comms — very specific scope and mandate
Cross-functional visibility across Policy, Legal, IR, Brand Social, and Engineering/Product
Explicitly states traditional comms/PR experience is NOT required — opens to non-traditional backgrounds
Emerging, high-visibility work area (agentic commerce, autonomous robotics) with real 'build in public' mandate
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous — JD references NYC-specific hiring tool (Covey AEDT) and 'remote jobs associated with office in NYC' but does not clearly specify hybrid vs. fully remote
Role requires high judgment and risk tolerance at a public company — pressure-intensive environment with significant scrutiny risk
No clear reporting structure or team size mentioned — organizational context is opaque

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, title 'Manager', and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' language in Covey notice)
location.work_auth (stated as open by absence of restriction; no explicit statement)
ambiguities: Location type is unclear: the JD mentions 'Notice to Applicants for Jobs Located in NYC or Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' suggesting possible NYC-based or NYC-remote hybrid, but no explicit city or work arrangement is stated for the role itself. Treated as 'unclear' with remote_us_eligible: true.
manages_people is inferred as false — title includes 'Manager' but no direct reports are mentioned; role appears to be a senior IC/player-coach based on responsibilities described.

Nice-to-haves section is empty: the JD does not explicitly list preferred/bonus qualifications separate from required ones. All listed qualifications in 'We're excited about you because…' use declarative/hard language and are captured as must_haves.
